title: Wholesome Herbal Norwegian Bread Soup
description: A light, herb-infused soup featuring airy bread puffs and fresh dill, served in a rustic earthenware bowl.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and carrot to the pot, sautéing until the vegetables soften and the onion turns translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for about thirty seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
Sprinkle the crushed caraway seeds into the pot, stirring to coat the vegetables evenly.
Pour in the vegetable broth and water, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the cubed stale rye bread to the simmering broth, stirring gently so the bread soaks without disintegrating.
Season with mustard, sea salt, black pepper, and the chopped dill, stirring to combine all flavors.
Reduce the heat slightly and allow the soup to simmer for an additional five minutes, letting the herbs infuse the liquid.
While the soup rests, toast the remaining bread cubes in a dry skillet over medium heat until they puff up and turn golden brown.
Ladle the hot soup into an earthenware bowl, top with the freshly toasted bread puffs and a few sprigs of fresh dill, then serve immediately.
